title: admetshiny 1.0.0
text:
admetshiny 1.0.0

Initial CRAN release.
Interactive Shiny application with four data-source modules: SwissADME, CDK & webchem, ADMETlab 3.0 and Deep-PK.
Drug-likeness filters: Lipinski, Veber, Ghose, Egan, Muegge.
BOILED-Egg model, PCA, t-SNE, parallel coordinates, violin, radar and correlation heatmap visualizations.
Tanimoto / AGNES structural-similarity clustering.
Dark mode, custom colour palettes and high-resolution figure export.
Codename: Espeletia hartwegiana.





 Back to top

title: admetshiny
text: Provides an interactive shiny application and a toolbox of R functions for the management, calculation, filtering, visualization and exploratory analysis of molecular descriptors and ADMET (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion and Toxicity) properties of small molecules. It integrates workflows from SwissADME, ADMETlab 3.0 and Deep-PK, computes descriptors locally via the Chemistry Development Kit (CDK), and offers drug-likeness filters (Lipinski, Veber, Ghose, Egan, Muegge), the BOILED-Egg model, PCA, t-SNE, radar plots and Tanimoto/AGNES clustering to support compound prioritization in early-stage drug discovery.

title: admetshiny
text: Provides an interactive shiny application and a toolbox of R functions for the management, calculation, filtering, visualization and exploratory analysis of molecular descriptors and ADMET (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion and Toxicity) properties of small molecules. It integrates workflows from SwissADME, ADMETlab 3.0 and Deep-PK, computes descriptors locally via the Chemistry Development Kit (CDK), and offers drug-likeness filters (Lipinski, Veber, Ghose, Egan, Muegge), the BOILED-Egg model, PCA, t-SNE, radar plots and Tanimoto/AGNES clustering to support compound prioritization in early-stage drug discovery.

title: About ADMETShiny
text:
With ADMETShiny, say goodbye to manual ADMET analysis although different Web-Servers. Now, you could management your physicochemical profiles in once only spot.

Finally, you could generate an once report about the principal characteristics of the each model.
If you’re not estimate that’s profile in external websites…, Don’t Worry!, This Shiny include a web-local-server built with rCDK R package and Java SE Development Kit (JDK).

title: About ADMETShiny
section: One for open source and open source for one
text:
One for open source and open source for one
Input data although of different methods (datasets in “.csv”, search in PubChem with CID, common name, inchKey & CAS).

Now, you can calculate drug-likeness properties with CDK & WebChem
The dataset & management filters could apply above to the properties (molecular descriptors) and get very easy reports.







Yes, you could use the console for develop a simple reproducible script
And this isn’t all! You could generate your reports with the console… :)

title: About ADMETShiny
section: The Shiny App for coders & no coders
text:
The Shiny App for coders & no coders
Built for both coders and non-coders, ADMETShiny offers an intuitive graphical interface that makes advanced cheminformatics analyses accessible without programming experience, while remaining fully integrated with the R ecosystem for users who require reproducible, customizable, and extensible workflows.

title: About ADMETShiny
text:
Knowledge structure analysis
ADMETShiny provides a comprehensive collection of interactive modules designed to streamline the early stages of drug discovery by integrating molecular retrieval, descriptor calculation, ADMET prediction, chemical space exploration, structural similarity analysis, and automated reporting into a single user-friendly environment.





Overall, ADMETShiny combines data retrieval, descriptor calculation, predictive modeling, interactive visualization, molecular prioritization, and automated reporting into a unified open-source platform specifically designed to accelerate computational drug discovery and medicinal chemistry research.

title: admetshiny
text:
How to install?
Two different forms for install this package..
The next steps:
Steps

Install R and RStudio.

You could choice your R & Rstudio version f your operative system.
I recommended install RStudio for the easyful user interface

Install the pak our remotes packages.
To enjoy all the features of ADMETShiny, we suggest you install all the dependencies listed below.

#All dependencies:
install.packages(c("shiny", "DT", "ggplot2", "dplyr", "magrittr", "rcdk", "webchem", "fmsb", "Rtsne", "ggrepel", "fingerprint", "cluster", "GGally", "openxlsx", "knitr", "rmarkdown", "testthat"))
Note: For the user it isn’t necessary the package “testthat”. However, if you could collaborate in the debugging and review of this packages, please, don’t doubt of send me your suggestions in the section “Feedback”.

For the “rCDK” & Webchem package is necessary the follow dependence:

Please, consider this option for the all features.
Java SE Development Kit Downloads by Oracle

Installation to the package in your environment with the follow steps:

You could consider install “pak”, follow the steps:
#Write in the R & RStudio console:
install.packages("pak")
#Call the package
library(pak)
#Follow this step: 
pak::pkg_install("xavierclementegarcia/ADMETShiny", subdir = "package")
You could install the package with “remotes”, :
#Write in the R & RStudio console:
install.packages("remotes")
#Call the package
library(remotes)
#Follow this step: 
remotes::install_github("xavierclementegarcia/ADMETShiny", subdir = "package", subdir = "package")
Remember that the correct proficiency of the Shiny app is required JDK & the others dependencies listing above.

Launch ADMETShiny.

#Call to the package
library("admetshiny")
#For run the Shiny app
admetshiny::run_app()

title: Introduction to admetshiny
text: admetshiny is an R package that provides an interactive Shiny application and a toolbox of functions for the management, calculation, filtering, visualization and exploratory analysis of molecular descriptors and ADMET properties of small molecules. It integrates data exported from SwissADME, ADMETlab 3.0 and Deep-PK, and can compute descriptors locally via the Chemistry Development Kit (CDK).

title: Introduction to admetshiny
text: admetshiny is an R package that provides an interactive Shiny application and a toolbox of functions for the management, calculation, filtering, visualization and exploratory analysis of molecular descriptors and ADMET properties of small molecules. It integrates data exported from SwissADME, ADMETlab 3.0 and Deep-PK, and can compute descriptors locally via the Chemistry Development Kit (CDK).

title: Introduction to admetshiny
text:
Launching the application
The easiest way to use admetshiny is through its interactive application:
admetshiny::run_app()
The application provides four modules:

SwissADME Manager - upload a SwissADME CSV and apply drug-likeness filters.
CDK & webchem - retrieve SMILES from PubChem and calculate descriptors locally.
ADMETlab 3.0 - upload an ADMETlab CSV; properties are mapped automatically.
Deep-PK - upload a Deep-PK CSV; missing descriptors are calculated on-the-fly from SMILES.

title: Introduction to admetshiny
text:
Using the functions programmatically
The exported functions can also be used in plain R scripts.

Drug-likeness filters
library(admetshiny)

# Build a small toy dataset in the SwissADME schema
d <- data.frame(
  MW = c(300, 650),
  LogP = c(2, 7),
  TPSA = c(40, 160),
  MR = c(70, 150),
  "#H-bond acceptors" = c(4, 12),
  "#H-bond donors" = c(2, 7),
  "#Rotatable bonds" = c(3, 14),
  "#Heavy atoms" = c(20, 80),
  "#Aromatic heavy atoms" = c(6, 9),
  check.names = FALSE
)

# Add the violation columns required by the filters
d <- computeViolationColumns(d)

# Apply Lipinski + Veber
filtered <- applyFilters(d, filters = c("Lipinski", "Veber"))


Normalizing a SwissADME export
d <- read.csv("swissadme.csv", check.names = FALSE)
d <- fixSwissADME(d)


BOILED-Egg plot
# Requires LogP and TPSA columns
plotBoiledEgg(d)

title: Introduction to admetshiny
text:
Optional dependencies
Some features rely on suggested packages that are not installed automatically:



Feature
Package




CDK descriptors / Deep-PK processing
rcdk (requires Java JDK)


SMILES from PubChem
webchem


Radar plot
fmsb


t-SNE
Rtsne, ggrepel


PCA labels
ggrepel


Tanimoto / AGNES
rcdk, fingerprint, cluster


Parallel coordinates
GGally


Excel export
openxlsx



Install them with:
install.packages(c("rcdk", "webchem", "fmsb", "Rtsne", "ggrepel",
                   "fingerprint", "cluster", "GGally", "openxlsx"))

title: Introduction to admetshiny
text:
References

Daina, A., Michielin, O., & Zoete, V. (2017). SwissADME. Scientific Reports, 7(1), 42717.
Daina, A., & Zoete, V. (2016). A boiled egg to predict gastrointestinal absorption and brain penetration. ChemMedChem, 11(11), 1117-1121.
Fu, L., et al. (2024). ADMETlab 3.0. Nucleic Acids Research, 52(W1), W422-W431.
Myung, Y., de Sa, A. G., & Ascher, D. B. (2024). Deep-PK. Nucleic Acids Research, 52(W1), W469-W475.
